Waterfront properties and multi-unit buildings may require more extensive treatment. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants common in Edgewater properties?
faqs.answer: Edgewater's location along the Hudson River creates high humidity levels that attract carpenter ants to moisture-damaged wood. The combination of older homes with wooden structures and newer buildings with landscaping, plus the area's mature trees, provides ideal nesting sites. Carpenter ants are particularly active from spring through fall in Edgewater's climate.

faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my Edgewater home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants in Edgewater are significantly larger than regular ants (up to 5/8 inch long) and are typically black or dark brown. Key signs include piles of wood shavings (frass) near baseboards or windowsills, rustling sounds inside walls, and seeing large winged ants indoors during spring. Unlike termites, carpenter ants have a narrow waist and elbowed antennae.
